events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves fall protection equipment with frayed/burned webbing. This is clearly defective and non-compliant. OSHA requires personal fall protection equipment to be inspected and removed from service if damaged. Frayed/burned webbing compromises the integrity of the harness/lanyard.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical (fall protection failure could lead to death)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded
Percent complete: maybe 80 since rough-complete
Confidence: high, 0.95

    "Frayed/burned webbing on fall protection equipment renders it unsafe for use",
    "Damaged webbing compromises load-bearing capacity and may fail under fall arrest forces",
    "Equipment does not meet inspection criteria for continued service"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove damaged fall protection equipment from service and tag out",
    "Replace with compliant, undamaged equipment before any work at height proceeds",
    "Inspect all other fall protection gear on site for similar damage",
    "Document defect and report to safety supervisor"
  ],
  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing on fall protection equipment is a clear defect that violates OSHA inspection requirements. Under both 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140, personal fall protection must be inspected before use and removed from service if damaged, defective, or showing signs of degradation. Burned or frayed webbing significantly reduces the strength of the strap and can cause catastrophic failure during a fall arrest event. This is a critical life-safety issue.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.502 - Fall protection systems criteria and practices",
    "OSHA 1926.951 - Safety belts, lifelines, and lanyards",
    "OSHA 1910.140 - Personal fall protection systems",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) - Equipment must be inspected before use and removed from service if damaged"
  ]
}
